news.text: Exclusive: Trump's Iran war pushes India to rekindle old friendship with Russia - Reuters
With 'no place to hide' traders spend sleepless nights as Iran war roils markets - Reuters
UK retail sales fall back in February ahead of Iran war impact - Reuters
UAE willing to join international force to reopen Strait of Hormuz, FT reports - Reuters
India cuts excise duties on petrol, diesel as global oil prices surge - Reuters
India's stock benchmarks post fifth straight weekly loss on continuing Iran war - Reuters

brief:
◆ Market Overview

Futures are bleeding red as the Iran war continues to shake global markets, with tech leading the carnage down 3.11% pre-market. VIX spiked 6.92% to 36.62, signaling serious fear as traders brace for another volatile session. The SPX is testing key support at $645 while oil volatility keeps risk-off sentiment front and center.

◆ What to Watch

  • Iran War Developments: Any escalation news will drive immediate volatility across energy and risk assets
  • Strait of Hormuz Reports: UAE joining international force could provide some relief to oil premium
  • Tech Weakness: XLK down over 3% - watch NVDA and mega-cap earnings reactions
  • Energy Rotation: XLE up 1.57% as only green sector - crudeInventories impact
⚡ Key Driver
Geopolitical risk premium is dominating price action. Energy remains the only safe haven sector as Iran conflict disrupts global trade routes.

◆ Sector Rotation

Classic risk-off rotation in full swing with energy (XLE +1.57%) and utilities (XLU +0.18%) catching bids while growth gets hammered. Tech's 3.11% pre-market drop shows institutional money fleeing duration risk amid geopolitical uncertainty. Communication services also bleeding heavily at -2.36%, suggesting broad-based risk asset liquidation.
⚠ Bear Case
Iran war escalation could trigger systematic deleveraging. With VIX at 36.62, we're one headline away from a serious gap down below key support levels.
✦ Bull Case
UAE joining Strait of Hormuz coalition signals coordinated international response. If oil stabilizes, oversold tech names could see aggressive dip buying into quarter-end.

◆ Levels That Matter

  • SPY $640: Critical support - break here opens door to $620 retest
  • QQQ $565: Must hold or we see accelerated tech selloff
  • VIX $40: Resistance level that signals panic buying if breached
  • XLE $95: Energy breakout level if geopolitical premium expands

Not a prediction. A preparation. Make your plan before the bell.
